name: protein_quality_baseline
description: Two-stage protein redesign on the streptococcal protein G B1 domain (GB1, 56 aa, PDB 2QMT; Gronenborn et al. 1991, Frericks Schmidt et al. 2007 for the high-resolution coordinates). Stage 1 uses rejection-sampling to broadly explore the local sequence neighborhood with ESM2-650M (Lin et al. 2023) at temperature 1.0 and a 3-mutation budget, retaining the top samples by ESMFold pLDDT. Stage 2 narrows to single-mutation MCMC at lower ESM2 temperature (0.7) and adds three additional constraints to discipline ESM2's tendency to drift toward low-complexity, high-pLDDT 'AAAEEK'-style sequences: `structure-ptm` rewards globally consistent fold prediction; `protein-complexity` (max_low_complexity 0.30) caps the SEG-style low-complexity fraction (Wootton & Federhen 1996); `balanced-aa` (min_aa_frequency=0.01, max_underrepresented_count=8) flags severe amino-acid collapse without penalizing the natural sparseness of a 56 aa protein (wild-type GB1 has 5 amino acids absent and 4 at 1.79% frequency, well within the relaxed thresholds). The thresholds are tuned for short-protein redesign; for full-length protein redesign use the constraint defaults (min_aa_frequency=0.02, max_underrepresented_count=3). Canonical baseline for the protein-quality teaching example.
